description: Shared skills and standards across all specialist templates

documentation.name: Documentation Standards
content: All files must include header comments with purpose, author, and last modified date
Inline comments for complex logic (not obvious operations)
API documentation for all public interfaces

code_quality.name: Code Quality Standards
content: Follow DRY principle - Don't Repeat Yourself
Functions should do ONE thing (Single Responsibility)
Maximum file length: 500 lines (split if larger)
Error handling for all external calls and edge cases
Logging for important operations and errors

security.name: Security Standards
content: Input validation for all user inputs
Principle of least privilege for permissions

version_control.name: Version Control Standards
content: Atomic commits - one logical change per commit

handoff.name: Phase Handoff Protocol
handoff.description: Standard procedure for transitioning between phases
steps: Summarize current phase achievements in 3-5 bullet points
List all artifacts created with file paths
Document decisions made and their rationale
Identify blockers or risks for next phase
Confirm acceptance criteria met
Get explicit user confirmation to proceed
Update state with phase completion

error_recovery.name: Error Recovery Protocol
steps: Log error with full context (timestamp, operation, input)
Assess severity: blocking, warning, or informational
Attempt automatic recovery if safe
Notify user of error with clear explanation
Provide recovery options
Create checkpoint before retry
Document resolution for future reference

context_management.name: Context Management
steps: Monitor estimated token usage
Prune completed phases to summaries when >80% limit
Keep only current phase in full detail
Archive detailed phase data to disk
Load templates selectively by relevance
Clear temporary calculation contexts

shell_commands.name: Shell Commands
restrictions: NEVER execute destructive operations without confirmation
NEVER run commands that modify system state without backup
ALWAYS validate inputs before shell execution

universal.name: Universal Boundaries
rules: NEVER execute destructive operations without explicit confirmation
ALWAYS respect user's authority on business/domain decisions
NEVER proceed with unclear requirements - ask for clarification
ALWAYS preserve existing working code unless explicitly asked to change
NEVER commit or push code without explicit confirmation
ALWAYS clean up temporary files after use

security_critical.name: Security Critical
rules: NEVER disable security features for convenience
NEVER use eval() or equivalent dangerous functions
NEVER trust user input without validation

progress_updates.frequency: Every 5-10 minutes or after major milestones
format: Progress indicator (X of Y complete, percentage)
